item.title: Agreed
item.description: Most viral edit: Thanks for the upvotes, my dudes! And for the record, I’m not an ally in the LGBTQ agenda, I’m an accomplice. Original post: Don’t have the source because I suck at switching between apps on iPhone and lost the page. Pride is year round, don’t let the calendar hold you back from being yourself. And as always, Be Gay and Do Crimes.

No joke, I remember reading this as a kid.
Matthew 18:9
Everything old is new again
No lies detected
Do this to them individually and make bank
Seriously, who doesn't think about Rachel Weisz?
Let's start a war, start a nuclear war, at the . . .
It's nice to be represented
Well, maybe not this year

item.title: Cartoon Memes Vol 1
item.description:
Okay MOSTLY Cartoons
I'm picking back up where I left off clearing out some of the memes I spent a frankly absurd amount of time organizing into folders on my phone. Hope you all enjoyed, as always I'll post the links to my previous posts by category below for anyone who's interested. Star Wars Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/SVzbsKr Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/agPBjZY Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/3WbYPiO Part 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/D3xLtoh Part 5: https://imgur.com/gallery/hOagKbC Lord of the Rings https://imgur.com/gallery/YrdND58 Marvel https://imgur.com/gallery/J243dDD Harry Potter https://imgur.com/gallery/FCOoDNo Disney https://imgur.com/gallery/VFswkF4 Marvel https://imgur.com/gallery/tgBY85X Anime https://imgur.com/gallery/4oTHG6o Gaming Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/tgBY85X Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/dQN08Rs Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/uZJkc2E Part 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/zo8IFb1 Musical Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/JiwKewf Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/t16zecY Animals Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/P0cGACu Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/2ohYMNV Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/9i0lkoD Part 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/0wD3uKa Workplace Memes Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/8DXcQeO Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/POhPf2K

item.title: Raise kids to be themselves and then be proud when they do exactly that! That's a parents job, yes grieve if you need to, yes take a moment, but be fucking proud you did a good enough job that they can tell you who they are! That's being an awesome parent
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: Yosemite Falls In Yosemite National Park California
item.description: Yosemite Falls is the highest waterfall in Yosemite National Park, dropping a total of 2,425 feet (739 m) from the top of the upper fall to the base of the lower fall. Located in the Sierra Nevada of California, it is a major attraction in the park, especially in late spring when the water flow is at its peak. #mountains #awesome #nature #hiking #the_great_outdoors
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: After the devastating earthquake and tsunami hit Japan in 2011, chaos swept across the country. At the heart of it all was the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant—damaged, unstable, and teetering on the edge of disaster. A monument to the workers
item.description: After the devastating earthquake and tsunami hit Japan in 2011, chaos swept across the country. At the heart of it all was the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant—damaged, unstable, and teetering on the edge of disaster. As radiation levels soared and the cooling systems failed, the risk of a full nuclear meltdown loomed large. And then, something remarkable happened. A group of about 50 workers—engineers, technicians, and operators, many of them older, some even retired—chose to stay behind. They weren’t soldiers. They didn’t carry weapons. But they walked straight into danger, knowing exactly what was at stake. They worked in shifts, wearing heavy protective gear, often unsure if they’d make it out alive. Their mission was simple, but incredibly risky: to pump seawater into the overheating reactors and prevent a total meltdown. Machines couldn’t do it. Robots couldn’t either. It had to be them. They became known as the Fukushima 50. These men and women weren’t looking for medals or headlines. They just knew someone had to do it. If they failed, the consequences could be catastrophic—not just for Japan, but for the world. Their courage wasn’t loud. It wasn’t flashy. But it was real. They showed up when everything seemed lost. They risked their lives to buy time, to protect others, and to stop something unimaginable from happening. To this day, many of them have never spoken publicly. Some suffered long-term health effects. But what they did can’t be forgotten. In a world full of noise, the quiet bravery of the Fukushima 50 still echoes.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: An example of the reasoning disconnect in groups searching for evidence of Vaccine death. This posted by BCRail, suffering from a lack of cognitive ability.
item.description:
So here it is: a study by a group that peer reviews itself showing exceptionally poor conclusions from questionable, non-present, or fabricated data and reviewed by themselves rather than independent peers: https://publichealthpolicyjournal.com/a-systematic-review-of-autopsy-findings-in-deaths-after-covid-19-vaccination/
As if a facebook link isnt the first clue this might be a sketchy source of review, lets look on. Medline doesnt mention any of the 3 journals the 'peers' are utilizing, along with the journal authors here, meaning it probably already isnt anything credible, but second, lacking publications in any of NEJIM, NIH, CJM or BMJ likely is because their definition of peer reviewed is thus as they get rejected from any credible journal. It isnt worth the waste of paper its' been printed on, in all probability. The critical thinking anyone with more than 3 firing brain cells needs to do is: not just to seek the OUTCOME you expect, but to look at science with a reputation for independent integrety and peer review. Moving on though, lets examine the claim above. 73.9% ! GOSH ! Thats a MAJORITY. But of what? Sudden deaths of WHAT GROUP STUDIED? The offered source holds this paper: https://publichealthpolicyjournal.com/a-systematic-review-of-autopsy-findings-in-deaths-after-covid-19-vaccination/ "We initially identified 678 studies and, after screening for our inclusion criteria, included 44 papers that contained 325 autopsy cases and one organ-restricted autopsy case (heart)." Which studies, and by what criteria did they pick 44 of the papers with the 325 autopsy cases they selected. Selectively. For the selection of the outcome they wanted. Confirmation bias, much? Im not going to break down WHY this seems to be, as a lot of nonsense is conveyed. It contradicts what other reputable publications have discovered through real peer reviewed science, so Ill leave the rest of that reading to you and instead back up my claim with real peer reviewed science. Specifically that on clinically verified deaths due to the COVID vaccine. Now this will be a low number, because some pople had other co-morbidities at time of death (other things wrong with them that COVID or a negative reaction, if any, to the vaccine or any other reason may have pushed them over the edge and caused death), but even if the real number is 10 or 100 times what we think we know it is (~55/8 BILLION) MINISCULE in relation to how many doses have been administered. Sauce: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8875435/ There are other studies, and you can look up those at NEJIM or anywhere else, but they find similar results so lets just run with this one. NIH has been counting deaths reported to coroner and from hospitals across the world and they found that out of 8 BILLION doses administered, 55 cases can be concretely tied to vaccine dosage. If you have a problem with the number - if its 55, 5500, or 55,000 , compared to the billions of doses given, its LESS than one percent, and by a good margin. Now, you HAVE to assess risk every day. Going out your front door, even, but you DO IT ANWAYS because its WORTH IT. The vaccine lowers symtoms and SAVES LIVES in OVER 99.999% of the people vaccinated. A lot better than the %0.7 to 3.5% chance of DEATH in clinical cases for COVID. Its like wearing your seatbelt. Can it kill you? YES ! REMOTELY ! But it has a FAR GERATER chance of SAVING YOUR LIFE in a crash if you wear it. This is the fault in reasoning with people taking a singular focus to validate their fear. The *IS* a chance of adverse reaction to vaccine. The error is assuming there isnt a FAR GREATER risk of harm from these diseases. This isnt a common cold or mild flu. Measeles vaccine is worth taking because of high infant mortality. Scarlet fever vaccine is worth taking to avoid heart damage. Mumps, Rubella, Diptheria and others are taken as their risks are far outwieghed by the consequences of catching their respective diseases and SO IS COVID. Unplug yourself from the facebook disinformation cabal and seek a better light. Get a more established (before 2020 as PHPJ is) and assure it is a REPUTABLE, independently peer reviewed publication, and then read MORE. Speak to MORE than one doctor. Dont just turn away when you dont find what you seek but look at all of them and then how the conclusions contradict or reinforce themselves. I wrote this book not just for @BCRail , but for everyone who is fearful of vaccines. Check your assessment of risk, and I think you can find some comfort and reassurance that it is, indeed the proper method to take versus the horrible diseases that assail us.
item.dc:creator: Bystandr

item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: Because it's important: a reminder of what the growing cells in the first 2-3 months of pregnancy actually look like
item.description: The forced birthers have successfully spread images of miniaturised babies as representation of what is happening in the womb in early pregnancy, when that couldn't be further from the truth. There isn't even a fetus yet at that point, let alone anything remotely resembling a human (or any being, period). Their tactic was and is to rile up an emotional response when telling lies, pushing through violent legislation, and pressuring and forcing people with a uterus to carry a pregnancy to term. There is no mini human at that point. There are just cells with the potential to grow into something that *then* has the potential to grow into a human. Having the choice to keep or to terminate is of utmost importance. And knowing what there actually exists during the relevant time is part of that choice.
item.dc:creator: abetteridiot

item.title: What Happened Today - #124
item.description: A democracy dies when systematic erasure makes entire communities disappear while everyone argues about social media posts. The government is stripping Harvey Milk's name from the ship commissioned in his honor, eliminating 90% of tribal college funding, and using collective punishment to deport entire families for crimes that their relatives committed. Our country is methodically disappearing the institutions and people that make America diverse, educated, and humane until only the approved remain visible. The void isn't an accident. It's the point. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/musk-disgusting-abomination 2. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-recission-9billion 3. https://go.govbrief.today/steel-tariffs-begin 4. https://go.govbrief.today/revoke-emergency-abortion 5. https://go.govbrief.today/prisons-hormone-therapy 6. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-renames-harvey-milk 7. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-skips-nato 8. https://go.govbrief.today/baraka-sues-habba 9. https://go.govbrief.today/biden-pardons-investigated 10. https://go.govbrief.today/democratic-fec-challenge 11. https://go.govbrief.today/doj-drops-navarro-suit 12. https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-raid-nazi 13. https://go.govbrief.today/chemical-safety-board 14. https://go.govbrief.today/humanitarian-parole-girl 15. https://go.govbrief.today/soliman-family-custody 16. https://go.govbrief.today/tribal-colleges-defunded 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-social-media-volume Yesterday's Story - https://go.govbrief.today/nadler-aide-investigation Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/resource-disappearing-people Get Free In Email Daily - https://govbrief.today

item.dc:creator: Shawnofavitch

item.title: My Maelle Cosplay from Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
item.description:
These shots were taken by Pao Images at MomoCon 2025. Please note I’m still in Act 2 so no spoilers pls :) I was originally going to do her Expedition outfit but then I unlocked Obscur and that’s what I’m going with now!! It will take me probably 6 months though because I no work a full time job and this is my little side hobby :) so in the meantime I’ve been rocking her Lumiere outfit! Reference photos of the character/skins below as well some WIPS of my pattern making for Obscur and a new version of her rapier (I’ve since reprinted a more accurate STL).
I can pass more of a likeness to her if i widen my eyes/maintain a relaxed expression - but since these shots were outside I couldn’t help but be squinty!
Lumiere outfit
Obscur outfit
Pattern making for the armor - traced over the screenshot in Inkscape and printed out (also this pic was taken in the mirror so it will be on the correct side of my body)
New version of Maellum - note the runes on the blade as well as refined blade shape, cross guard shape, etc. this one is also more proportional than v1. STL by KosmoCraft on Cults3D
item.dc:creator: LittleLuckyCosplay

item.title: Dump of amazing things, sometimes funny, sometimes amazing. Enjoy !
item.description:
On a garbage truck this morning
A ladder bike slowly overtaking a bunk bed.
Books Saved by Superheroes
Folding up the tarp after a rain delay .
Home Alone 2030.
1934 Edsel Ford Model 40 Special Speedster
Cashier counting money.
In a store window in Beijing China. I think something got lost in translation (maybe).
Sky ladder on the Qixing Mountain, China.
There's just something special about breaking ice.
Shearing a rug to give it a cleaner look.
Pebble art rock.
Spotted on the London Underground (photo credit: everittmatt)
Opening the cascading floodgates of a dam
wash windows.
Manual vacuum cleaners.
Chocolate + Espresso = Love
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot
